title: swatplus-builder: Claim-governed SWAT+ hydrologic modeling from a USGS gauge ID
description:
swatplus-builder builds and calibrates SWAT+ hydrologic models in Python, starting from a single USGS streamgage ID. A locked benchmark calibration protocol (lock → calibrate → independently verified rerun) ensures that reported metrics always come from a clean rerun, never the optimizer loop. Runtime gates assign each result a claim tier (exploratory → diagnostic → research_grade → publication_grade) and write a machine-readable evidence bundle — including typed, evidence-backed refusals — so both successes and limitations are inspectable.

The pipeline runs entirely in Python with no desktop GIS (no QGIS, PyQGIS, or QSWATPlus plugin). GIS operations use WhiteboxTools, rasterio, and geopandas. The package exposes an 11-tool MCP surface for AI agent operation and a full CLI (swat workflow run).

Key contribution: the software, not the operator, decides what each result is allowed to claim. A strong metric never promotes itself past a failed gate.
